default.summary: Electrolyzer and balance-of-plant supply chains are global; delivery and lead times vary. Proximity to ports and industrial hubs helps. Confirm supplier lead times for your timeline.

CA.summary: Major ports (LA/Long Beach) for equipment; strong industrial and tech base. Electrolyzer and BOP suppliers serve West Coast. Plan for lead times and logistics.

TX.summary: Gulf ports and industrial corridor; oil and gas supply chain overlaps with H2 equipment. Strong logistics and manufacturing base for project delivery.

NM.summary: Rail and I-25 to Denver and El Paso; proximity to solar and industrial hubs. Equipment typically via regional distributors or direct shipment.

AZ.summary: I-10 and rail to California and Mexico; solar and industrial logistics. Supplier access via West Coast and Southwest hubs.

NV.summary: I-80 and rail; connections to California and Salt Lake. Equipment delivery via West Coast ports and regional hubs.

WA.summary: Puget Sound ports; rail to Midwest. Industrial and clean-tech supply chain. Electrolyzer and BOP via West Coast.

OR.summary: Port of Portland; I-5 corridor. Equipment and components via West Coast logistics.

WY.summary: Rail and I-80; wind and industrial corridor. Delivery from Denver, SLC, or Gulf. Plan for lead times.

UT.summary: I-15/I-80; rail to West Coast and Denver. Regional hub for equipment and logistics.

CO.summary: Front Range logistics; rail and I-25. Growing H2 and renewable supply chain; supplier presence increasing.

IA.summary: Rail hub; central location. Wind and ag equipment supply chain; electrolyzer delivery from Gulf or Midwest.

OK.summary: Pipeline and rail; Cushing and Gulf links. Energy equipment and logistics established.

KS.summary: I-70 and rail; central corridor. Equipment via Kansas City and Gulf. Confirm lead times.

IL.summary: Chicago rail and logistics hub; Great Lakes ports. Major equipment and manufacturing supply chain. Strong delivery options.

NY.summary: Port of NY/NJ; Northeast corridor. Industrial and clean-energy supply chain. Equipment via East Coast ports.

PA.summary: Ohio River and East Coast access; rail and highway. Manufacturing and energy equipment logistics.

OH.summary: DOE H2Hub; manufacturing and logistics hub. Strong electrolyzer and BOP supplier access. Cleveland and Cincinnati corridors.

FL.summary: Gulf and Atlantic ports; space coast and industrial logistics. Equipment via Gulf and East Coast.

LA.summary: Gulf ports and petrochemical corridor; major equipment and fabrication base. Strong supply chain for H2 and industrial projects.

ND.summary: Rail to Midwest and Canada; wind supply chain. Equipment delivery from regional hubs; plan for lead times.

NE.summary: I-80 and rail; central location. Ag and energy equipment logistics. Delivery from Gulf or Midwest.

MT.summary: Rail corridors; regional logistics. Equipment typically via Denver, SLC, or Pacific Northwest. Confirm lead times.
